## Key Points

- Board Chair DeAngelis announced key upcoming dates: a public budget forum (January 22), budget workshops (January 26, February 23), and a preliminary discussion with the city council (February 10), emphasizing that the school budget represents 61% of property taxes
- Chair stated this will be "another difficult budget year" requiring creative cost-saving ideas and broad community input, including from non-parent taxpayers
- The district is searching for a new superintendent, with interim superintendent George Entwistle currently serving; the board voted unanimously to allow student representatives to participate in semifinalist interviews under strict confidentiality conditions
- Finance committee reported the district is tracking toward a deficit for the current fiscal year (FY26), with 92% of funds expended/encumbered at the 50% mark of the year
- Board approved becoming a "community school district," expanding the community school strategy from the middle school to other schools, with potential state grant funding of ~$100K/year and an education foundation in development
- ICE enforcement activity was a major topic: board members and public speakers raised concerns about immigrant students missing school out of fear; the board unanimously endorsed the city council's resolution on federal immigration enforcement and community safety
- The board approved a motion requesting the superintendent produce a formal elementary school reconfiguration proposal for the March 2 budget workshop; Chair DeAngelis spoke strongly in favor while member Feller urged extensive community input
- Teachers' association president Sarah Gay asked for meaningful staff engagement (not just surveys) in budget planning and urged the board to seek a specific tax-increase target from the city council
- Member Feller suggested the tax increase may need to exceed the 6% guidance and advocated exploring early retirement buyouts for the 41 eligible staff, estimating potential savings over $1 million
- Pre-K update: district joined CDS cohort three for four-year-old services starting July 2026; currently 64 seats in South Portland schools plus 15/16 at Chickadee partnership; exploring Headstart classroom partnership
